title: In CXXRTL, in some cases, always_ff blocks are evaluated despite the sensitivity conditions not being met
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.37+21 (git sha1 3d9e44d18, clang 10.0.0-4ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Hi there!

After #4146 I have been running designs with two calls to `step()`, and noticed a new issue.
It seems that in some cases, always_ff blocks are evaluated despite the sensitivity conditions not being met.

[Here is a repository to help you reproduce the issue](https://github.com/flaviens/cxxrtl-b5). The repository also contains a test for Verilator for comparison to convince myself that this is correct.

Please let me know if you need any help reproducing the issue, or if you need any more information.

Thank you!
Flavien

### Expected Behavior

The always_ff block should not set the value of the output signal to 1

### Actual Behavior

The always_ff block sets the value of the output signal to 1
